_comment: Fabric Eventstream configuration for routing SQL Database change events.
This file documents (not deploys) the Eventstream topology that connects
the OLTP simulator's writes to real-time analytics.  The actual Eventstream
is created via Terraform (infra/modules/fabric-eventstream/) and configured
manually in the Fabric portal or via REST API.
OLTP Simulator → Fabric SQL Database → Change Event Streaming → Eventstream
└─→ Lakehouse (bronze layer)     — near-real-time CDC for batch analytics
The IoT sensor telemetry follows a separate path:
IoT Simulator (streaming/) → Event Hub → Eventstream → Eventhouse
description: Routes SQL Database CDC change events to Eventhouse and Lakehouse for real-time and batch analytics

sources.name: tt-sql-db-changes
sources.description: Change events from the Tales & Timber OLTP database (Fabric SQL Database)

tables.description: POS transaction headers — new sales created by the OLTP simulator
Line items for each POS transaction
Stock-level changes from sales and replenishment
CRM interactions (calls, emails, chats, returns, etc.)

config._note: Change Event Streaming captures inserts and updates automatically. Deletes are not expected in this OLTP workload.

sources.name: tt-iot-events
sources.description: IoT sensor telemetry from the streaming container (separate data path)

transformations.name: enrich-transactions
transformations.description: Add processing timestamp and source tag to transaction events

transformations.name: enrich-inventory
transformations.description: Add processing metadata to inventory change events

destinations.name: eventhouse-realtime
destinations.description: Real-time analytics — powers KQL dashboards and Reflex triggers

destinations.name: lakehouse-bronze
destinations.description: Near-real-time CDC landing zone — feeds the medallion architecture

step1_enableChangeEventStreaming: 1. Open the Fabric portal → navigate to tt_operational_db
2. Click 'Change Event Streaming' in the database settings
3. Select the tables: Transactions, TransactionItems, Inventory, CustomerInteractions
step2_configureEventstream: 1. Open the Eventstream 'tt-change-events' in the Fabric portal
2. Verify the SQL Database source appears with 4 tables
4. Map each source table to its destination table (see tableMappings above)
6. Map each source table to its bronze Delta path
step3_verifyDataFlow: 1. Run the OLTP simulator: cd simulator && python oltp_simulator.py --dry-run
2. Switch to live mode with a real SQL connection string
3. Open KQL Database → query transactions_realtime to see events arriving
4. Check Lakehouse bronze tables for Delta files landing
